title: Rosa subduplicata
intro: Rosa subduplicata, also known as the wild rose, is a perennial shrub belonging to the Rosaceae family. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, skin irritations, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Rosa subduplicata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat respiratory issues, menstrual cramps, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Rosa subduplicata is a species of rose in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Rosa, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as roses. Rosa subduplicata is likely classified as a member of the order Rosales, which includes the families Rosaceae and some other related plant families.
